methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the formal UW Regulation 2-114 PDF, Academic Affairs AI Committee resources, and several course- or report-level PDFs.

methodsAttempted.query: site:uwyo.edu "generative AI" "University of Wyoming" library citation privacy security
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found UW Libraries AI Support pages and several college/course-specific materials.
Direct HTTP checks confirmed robots.txt allowed the selected Academic Affairs HTML/PDF paths; selected paths are not in the disallow list.
Direct HTTP checks confirmed robots.txt allows the selected LibGuides paths under the general user-agent rules, with crawl-delay respected.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a formal University of Wyoming academic dishonesty regulation that explicitly addresses AI-based applications, an Academic Affairs AI Committee resource page, and UW Libraries AI Support guidance for citing AI, student use, and faculty syllabus/assignment design. Historical recommendations, externally provided report guidance, and course-specific syllabus materials were recorded as rejected rather than used as university-wide policy evidence.

stopConditions: Stop after verified official sources cover formal AI academic-integrity rules and current university library/Academic Affairs AI guidance.
Reject course-specific, historical report-only, external, or low-specificity materials as claim evidence.

artifacts.claimText: University of Wyoming Regulation 2-114 treats use of Artificial Intelligence-based applications as prohibited in cheating and fraud contexts unless the instructor expressly permits the use as specified.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Use Generative AI chatbots as a supplementary tool rather than a primary source. Avoid copy-pasting chatbot responses into your work. Not only might the response be factually incorrect, but copying without attribution can potentially be considered plagiarism. Always check with your instructor before using an AI chatbot to assist in your assignments, as instructor attitudes regarding its use can vary.

artifacts.title: University of Wyoming AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: The staged University of Wyoming bundle uses official sources only. The strongest formal source is UW Regulation 2-114, which addresses AI-based applications in academic dishonesty and plagiarism. Current Academic Affairs and UW Libraries sources provide AI resource, citation, student, and faculty guidance; these are scoped as guidance or source-status rather than binding policy. Historical, external, and course-specific candidates were rejected as claim evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.

UW Libraries and Academic Affairs resource pages are treated as official guidance or source-status context, not as binding university-wide policy unless the source language itself states a requirement.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
